rationale: The Iran deal inching closer is the most policy-relevant development for US residents — it involves direct US diplomacy, potential sanctions relief, and oil market implications; China's arrest of a US academic adds a new friction point.

top_events.title: Iran deal 'never been closer' — US Congressman warns against money flows
top_events.summary: The Iran deal is advancing with direct US involvement; a deal could lower oil prices and reduce Middle East tension, but domestic political opposition is vocal and the outcome remains uncertain.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for espionage
top_events.summary: Beijing's detention of a US academic escalates the pattern of US-China friction and raises concerns for Americans traveling to or working with China, adding pressure on bilateral relations.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: Putin's admission is relevant to US policymakers assessing the effectiveness of Ukraine support, but has limited direct impact on daily American life this cycle.
